current_fact.fact_text: On April 24, 1990, the Space Shuttle Discovery released the Hubble Space Telescope into orbit, placing a 2.4‑meter observatory above Earth’s atmosphere for the first time in history. Despite early optical flaws, Hubble went on to revolutionize astronomy with crystal-clear images of distant galaxies, nebulae, and the expansion of the universe.

episode.title: Morning Drops #03 - Space Shift
episode.description: Gaia invites you to shift your mindset with a simple yet powerful reflection: What if we saw emptiness not as a void but as space to create? An energising and feel-good selection of tracks, the perfect way to ease into your day and set the tone for the week ahead.
songs: Willy J Healey - Woke Up Smiling
The Strokes - You Only Live Once

current_fact.match_reason: A telescope that turns empty space into discovery pairs with an episode about reimagining emptiness as space to create.

queue.fact_text: In 1916, Ernest Shackleton and five crew members launched a small lifeboat from desolate Elephant Island on a near-impossible 1,300‑kilometre voyage through the Southern Ocean. Their goal was to bring rescue to the men stranded after the Endurance sank, a feat of navigation and endurance still considered one of the greatest survival journeys ever undertaken.

episode.title: Morning Drops #33 - Immerse
episode.description: An analogy between diving, one of Gaia's great passions, and the act of immersing in life itself. A reflection on what happens when we go below the surface: when we let ourselves be fully present. From moments of calm suspension to warm, rhythmic flow, the selection moves like water — fluid, organic, and alive. An invitation to slow down, listen deeper, and float.
songs: Pino D'Angiò - Un Po' d'Uva e un Liquore

queue.match_reason: An Antarctic open‑boat crossing echoes the episode’s themes of immersion in water, calm under pressure, and fluid journeys.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1914, James Franck and Gustav Hertz presented an experiment that showed atoms absorb energy in discrete jumps rather than smooth amounts. Their mercury-vapour experiments provided one of the first direct confirmations that electrons occupy quantized energy levels, helping solidify the strange new framework of quantum mechanics.

episode.title: Morning Drops #42 - The Joy Of Learning
episode.description:
Hosted by Yasmin, this Morning Drop explores learning as a lifelong process, focusing on persistence, repetition, and the emotional landscape that comes with challenging oneself over time.

Through personal stories and scientific insights, the episode looks at how learning reshapes the brain, generates joy through effort, and why progress often arrives after confusion and frustration. It reflects on the so-called delayed pleasure effect, the role of dopamine in motivation, and how curiosity and learning support long-term wellbeing.

The episode also questions our cultural relationship with failure and invites a softer, more realistic approach to learning, one that accepts mistakes as part of growth and sees persistence as a quiet form of strength.

Mood: reflective, grounded, quietly hopeful
Music genre: alternative rock, hip hop, electronic, neo-soul, drill

A space to listen slowly and stay with the process.

queue.match_reason: A painstaking experiment that reshaped physics fits an episode about the slow, often frustrating joy of learning and discovery.

queue.fact_text: In 1967, Soviet cosmonaut Vladimir Komarov became the first human to die during a space mission when his Soyuz 1 capsule’s parachute failed on reentry. His tragic flight exposed critical flaws in the rushed Soviet spacecraft program and became a somber reminder that the space race was paid for in human lives as well as technological triumphs.

episode.title: Morning Drops #45 - It Can't Rain Forever
episode.description:
In this first broadcast of 2026, Gaia returns from the vivid landscapes of Morocco to open a space for reflection. This episode navigates the delicate transition between closing a significant chapter and stepping into the new year. It explores the concept of constancy amidst change, celebrating the grounding force of friendship and the creative sanctuary that is Sedna FM.

The musical selection mirrors this journey, moving from the orchestral depth of changing seasons into the golden warmth of psychedelic soul. It recalls memories of Swiss mountains and unexpected downpours, reminding us that shared optimism can weather any storm.

Take a moment to breathe in the new morning and listen deeply.

Mood: Reflective, Warm, Transitional, Hopeful
Music genre: Classic Rock, Psychedelic Soul, Pop Rock, Soft Rock

queue.match_reason: A story of loss and lessons from a fatal mission pairs with a reflective episode about weathering storms and moving through grief toward hope.

queue.fact_text: On April 24, 1970, China launched its first satellite, Dong Fang Hong I, making it the fifth country to independently place an object in orbit. The satellite broadcast the patriotic song “The East Is Red” from space, turning a piece of orbital hardware into a political loudspeaker heard back on Earth.

episode.title: Morning Drops #24 - The Rise Of Afrobeat
episode.description: An exploration of the rise of Afrobeat, tracing its roots and evolution as a genre that blends African rhythms with jazz, funk, and highlife. A celebration of the infectious energy and cultural significance of this influential musical movement.

queue.match_reason: A satellite broadcasting a song into space resonates with an episode about music as a vehicle for political identity and national pride.

queue.fact_text: In 1922, the first segment of the Imperial Wireless Chain opened, linking Leafield in England to Cairo via long-distance radio. This high‑power network stitched together vast parts of the British Empire through invisible electromagnetic waves, turning the air itself into an empire‑wide telegraph line.

episode.title: Evening Flows #00 - Introduction to evening flows
episode.description:
Sedna FM introduces a new format: Evening Flow, where you can enjoy curated music sets from selectors, DJs, and artists.

Each mix is an invitation to drift beyond the expected. A space where hidden gems from personal archives come to light, where sound speaks its own language. There are no rules here, no patterns to follow. Just the steady unfolding of a mood, a texture, a journey shaped by feeling.

Let the music lead the way - enjoy the trip!

queue.match_reason: A wireless telegraphy chain turning the sky into a communication network mirrors Sedna’s own airwave journeys and the launch of a new radio format.

queue.fact_text: The Bandung Conference concluded on this day in 1955, gathering 29 Asian and African countries that had recently shaken off colonial rule. Their final communiqué condemned colonialism and racism and quietly laid the groundwork for the Non‑Aligned Movement—a bloc that insisted on a path beyond the Cold War’s two superpower camps.

episode.title: Morning Drops #39 - Moving Meditation
episode.description: An episode inspired by how travel helps us see more clearly. Stepping into new places opens the mind, shifts perspective. The music moves through balearic warmth, boogie grooves, world and latin-folk influences, and touches of new wave — a selection built to follow the quiet changes that happen when you're out of your usual rhythm.
songs: España Circo Este - Dormo poco e sogno molto
Talking Heads - This Must Be the Place (Naive Melody)

queue.match_reason: A conference of nations reimagining their place in the world fits an episode about travel, shifting perspectives, and finding new paths beyond old frameworks.

queue.fact_text: On April 24, 1944, the British Special Boat Service launched a surprise raid on the German garrison on Santorini, a volcanic island in the Aegean Sea. Operating in small teams under cover of darkness, they turned the island’s dramatic cliffs and coves into a covert battlefield in the wider war for the Mediterranean.

episode.title: Morning Drops #11 - Mediterranean Boat
episode.description: Gaia invites you to embark on a musical voyage across the Mediterranean. Exploring music from different countries shared by people who call them home. A warm, sun-soaked journey through coastal sounds.
songs: Fabrizio De André - Crêuza de mä

queue.match_reason: A secret World War II raid on a Greek island in the Aegean aligns with an episode built around Mediterranean seas, boats, and coastal stories.

queue.fact_text: In 1994, a decades-old Douglas DC‑3 suffered engine failure shortly after takeoff from Sydney and was forced to ditch in Botany Bay. Remarkably, the crew brought the vintage aircraft down on water so gently that all 25 people on board survived, turning a potential disaster into a textbook example of controlled ditching.

episode.title: Morning Drops #05 - Electric Shock
episode.description: An energizing episode with electrifying sounds to wake you up and get you moving. High energy tracks that deliver a jolt of musical electricity to start your day.

queue.match_reason: The sudden drama of an emergency water landing and a narrow escape mirrors the episode’s high-voltage energy and adrenaline-charged rock.

queue.fact_text: Gruinard Island off the coast of Scotland was officially declared free of anthrax on April 24, 1990, after 48 years of quarantine. The island had been deliberately contaminated during secret World War II biological weapons tests, and its decontamination required soaking the soil with thousands of liters of formaldehyde solution.

episode.title: Morning Drops #34 - Under Scottish Skies
episode.description: Gaia shares a personal moment of transition — a shift in seasons, in rhythm, and in life. From this space of quiet change comes a musical journey through Scotland: not the usual names, but voices that speak from the edges. A personal ritual and a small act of curiosity: an invitation to notice what grounds us when everything moves.
songs: Paolo Nutini - Pencil Full of Lead

queue.match_reason: A dark Scottish island finally cleansed of a wartime experiment fits an episode set under Scottish skies, balancing heavy history with grounded, reflective music.

queue.fact_text: Operation Eagle Claw, launched in 1980 to rescue American hostages in Tehran, ended in disaster when a sandstorm, mechanical failures, and a ground collision in the Iranian desert destroyed aircraft and killed eight servicemen. The failed mission exposed the complexities of long-range special operations and led directly to major reforms in U.S. special forces and joint command.

episode.title: Morning Drops #49 - Rage Against The Machine
episode.description:
In this Morning Drop, Yasmin revisits the story of Rage Against the Machine — a band born in tension, shaped by conviction, and remembered for moments that blurred the line between stage and protest.
From early Los Angeles unrest to Wall Street performances, this episode moves through rupture, resistance, and legacy — carried by songs that still feel urgent today.
Listen closely.
songs: Rage Against the Machine - Killing in the Name
Rage Against the Machine - Without a Face
Rage Against the Machine - Guerrilla Radio
Rage Against the Machine - Sleep Now in the Fire

queue.match_reason: A politically charged, failed rescue mission connects with an episode centered on Rage Against the Machine’s critique of power, war, and state violence.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1970, The Gambia became a republic within the Commonwealth, replacing Queen Elizabeth II with Dawda Jawara as its first president. In a single constitutional step, the small West African country reshaped its relationship with its former colonial ruler while remaining within a shared international framework.

episode.title: Morning Drops #24 - The Rise Of Afrobeat
episode.description: An exploration of the rise of Afrobeat, tracing its roots and evolution as a genre that blends African rhythms with jazz, funk, and highlife. A celebration of the infectious energy and cultural significance of this influential musical movement.

queue.match_reason: A West African nation redefining its political identity aligns with an episode celebrating West African musical movements tied to independence and self-definition.

queue.fact_text: Thorvald Stauning became Denmark’s prime minister for the first time in 1924, leading one of the world’s earliest social‑democratic governments. Under his leadership, Denmark pioneered welfare state policies that would later become a defining feature of Nordic societies.

episode.title: Morning Drops #01 - Timeless Legacy
episode.description: Gaia guides us through timeless music to start the morning with the right foot! The first episode of the Morning Drops series — classic sounds that never fade.
songs: Roberta Flack - Killing me softly with his song

queue.match_reason: A pioneering social‑democratic leader whose reforms became a lasting model fits an episode about legacies and classic creations that stand the test of time.

queue.fact_text: On April 24, 2011, WikiLeaks began publishing secret U.S. military documents about detainees at Guantanamo Bay. The files revealed how people were classified as threats, held indefinitely, and sometimes detained on the basis of flimsy or mistaken intelligence, intensifying global debates about transparency and security.

episode.title: Morning Drops #27 - Nomophobia
episode.description: Yasmin reflects on what happened when she recently lost her phone and why it felt freeing. A dive into the psychology of our relationship with smartphones: from nomophobia (the fear of being without a phone), to digital burnout and cognitive overload, to the surprising benefits of boredom and the brain's default mode network. An invitation to rethink how we connect and disconnect.

queue.match_reason: A leak that weaponized digital networks to expose hidden power structures matches an episode questioning our digital systems and how they shape perception and control.

queue.fact_text: In 2004, the United States lifted long-standing economic sanctions on Libya after the country renounced its weapons of mass destruction programs. The move closed a chapter of clandestine nuclear proliferation and signaled how dramatically a state’s status can shift when it trades secret arsenals for international reintegration.

episode.title: Sedna FM #02 - Destination Sedna
episode.description: Continuing the Sedna FM journey together with family and friends! Episode two of the main series — exploring new musical territories and deepening the connection with our listeners. A collective voyage through sound.
songs: Odion Iruoje - Identify With Your Roots
Depeche mode - world in my eyes (Oil Tank Mix)

queue.match_reason: A geopolitical pivot from isolation to reintegration fits with an episode about journeys, changing destinations, and new connections across borders.

queue.fact_text: Civil war erupted in the Dominican Republic on April 24, 1965, when Colonel Francisco Caamaño led a revolt to restore deposed president Juan Bosch. What began as a constitutionalist uprising quickly drew in U.S. Marines, turning a Caribbean power struggle into a Cold War flashpoint.

episode.title: Morning Drops #44 - Residente
episode.description:
In this edition of Morning Drops, Yasmin navigates the complex geography of René Pérez Joglar, known to the world as Residente. His story is not just a musical evolution; it is a timeline marked by resistance, lyrical obsession, and a relentless search for identity.

From the gated streets of Trujillo Alto to the global stage, we explore how a restless mind transformed into the voice of a generation. The selection moves through the textures of his life: hearing the raw energy of his early rebellion, the sharp precision of his political critique, and finally, the fragile silence of his most personal confessions.

It is a study in duality. We witness the rapper who dismantles giants and the son who misses his mother. It is a soundscape that proves that sometimes, the loudest revolution happens inside.

Mood: Raw, Revolutionary, Introspective, Honest
Music genre: Latin Alternative, Hip Hop, Urban, World

queue.match_reason: A Latin American civil conflict over legitimacy and sovereignty aligns with an episode about a Puerto Rican artist whose work dissects power, revolution, and identity.

queue.fact_text: The Suez Canal reopened on this date in 1957 after the Suez Crisis, once UN peacekeepers took up positions in Egypt. The 193‑kilometre waterway, briefly blocked by war, resumed its role as one of the planet’s most strategic trade arteries, re‑connecting oceans and economies.

episode.title: Morning Drops #11 - Mediterranean Boat
episode.description: Gaia invites you to embark on a musical voyage across the Mediterranean. Exploring music from different countries shared by people who call them home. A warm, sun-soaked journey through coastal sounds.
songs: Fabrizio De André - Crêuza de mä

queue.match_reason: The reopening of a Mediterranean canal that reshapes global routes fits an episode built around seafaring, coastal crossings, and the shared sea between continents.

queue.fact_text: On April 24, 1916, Irish rebels seized key buildings in Dublin, proclaimed an Irish Republic, and launched the Easter Rising against British rule. Though the revolt was crushed within a week, its leaders’ executions transformed public opinion and set Ireland on a path toward independence.

episode.title: Morning Drops #51 - The Dancefloor
episode.description:
For International Women’s Day, this Morning Drop turns its focus to women on the dancefloor, not as a trend, but as a cultural force.

Across five tracks, Gaia follows a line from disco closers to hip hop club classics, tracing how the dancefloor became a place where women could take space, build confidence, and create connection, sometimes with friends, sometimes alone, always with music at the centre.

This episode isn’t about pointing fingers. It’s a gentle reminder that spaces like the dancefloor still matter: places where people can express themselves, meet each other with respect, and feel part of something bigger than a night out.

Tracklist:
Donna Summer - Last Dance (1978)
The Sequence  - Funk you up
Salt-N-Pepa - Push It
Queen Latifah - Ladies First
Alicia Bridges - I Love the Nightlife

queue.match_reason: An uprising about reclaiming public space and self-determination aligns with an episode that frames the dancefloor as a site of empowerment and collective presence.

queue.fact_text: In 1885, sharpshooter Annie Oakley was hired by showman Nate Salsbury to join Buffalo Bill’s Wild West show. Her uncanny marksmanship and stage charisma turned a rural girl from Ohio into one of the world’s first female superstars of popular entertainment, challenging Victorian expectations of how a woman could move, dress, and perform.

episode.title: Morning Drops #36 - Women of Rock
episode.description: A tribute to the women who shaped the sound of rock, the ones who picked up a guitar and refused to ask for permission. The conversation unfolds in Italian, led by Gaia and her father. A dialogue between generations and a reminder that the pulse of rock has always belonged to anyone brave enough to play it loud. Distortion, rhythm, freedom.
songs: Joan Jett & The Blackhearts - I Love Rock 'n Roll

queue.match_reason: A 19th‑century woman redefining what was possible with a rifle parallels rock women breaking norms with guitars and amplifiers.

queue.fact_text: According to the ancient scholar Eratosthenes, April 24 marks the traditional date of the Fall of Troy, bringing an end to the legendary Trojan War. Whether or not a single city’s fall inspired Homer’s epics, this imagined burning city became a cornerstone myth about fate, pride, and the fragile line between history and storytelling.

episode.title: Morning Drops #38 - History of Flamenco
episode.description: A special journey into the history of Flamenco, guided by Yasmin and her guest Almudena from Madrid, Spain. Exploring the roots and evolution of this emotionally raw and culturally rich tradition, from its early vocal origins and Andalusian influences, through its transformation on stage and screen, to its modern reinventions by iconic Spanish artists.
songs: La Niña de los Peines - Al Gurugú
Paco de Lucía - Entre dos aguas
Camarón de la Isla - La leyenda del tiempo

queue.match_reason: A mythic war transmitted through oral poetry pairs well with an episode about flamenco—another tradition where stories, sorrow, and legend are sung into collective memory.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 2007, astronomers announced the discovery of Gliese 581c, one of the first known exoplanets lying in its star’s habitable zone and a serious candidate for a rocky, potentially Earth‑like world. Orbiting a red dwarf 20 light‑years away, it helped shift our imagination from ‘are there other worlds?’ to ‘how many?’

episode.title: Evening Flows #01 - Yasmin - Crescendo in the Medina
episode.description: The first Evening Flow episode comes from Yasmin. It starts deep in the medina, slow, mysterious, textured, and grows into a liberating dance across sub-Saharan landscapes. A journey through ambient soundscapes, Arabic melodies, Afro-Latin rhythms, and funky highlife grooves.
songs: David Visan & Carlos Campos - Crystal Heart
Evan Mars - Ya Zareef Al Tool
Dele Sosimi & Medlar - You No Fit Touch Am (Medlar Remix)

queue.match_reason: A new potentially habitable world around a distant star fits an evening mix that drifts from mysterious spacey textures into global, orbiting rhythms.

queue.fact_text: On April 24, 2013, the Rana Plaza garment factory building collapsed in Bangladesh, killing over 1,100 workers in one of the deadliest industrial disasters in history. The tragedy exposed the hidden human cost of fast fashion and spurred global conversations about supply chains, safety standards, and the price behind cheap clothes.

episode.title: Morning Drops #08 - (un)Boxing
episode.description: An episode about breaking free from expectations and labels. Music that helps us unbox ourselves and embrace who we truly are.
songs: Kendrick Lamar - The Blacker the Berry

queue.match_reason: A disaster that forced the world to confront the labels and systems behind consumer goods matches an episode about unboxing identities and questioning the structures we accept.

queue.fact_text: On April 24, 1982, the IBM PC’s open architecture ignited a wave of ‘IBM compatible’ clones, turning a single corporate design into a global platform. This unexpected openness helped standardize personal computers and quietly shaped how billions of people would work, play, and listen in the decades to come.

episode.title: Morning Drops #54 - Intentional Listening
episode.description:
Do you remember what it felt like to listen to music without a streaming platform?
In this Morning Drops episode, Gaia reflects on a recent experiment: stepping away from algorithmic listening and going back to an iPod. 

What starts as a small personal choice opens into a wider meditation on attention, presence, and what happens when music is no longer treated as something infinite, but as something we choose with care.

Tracklist:
Scientist - Drum Song Dub
The Other People Place - Let Me Be Me
A Forest Mighty Black - Fresh In My Mind
Cymande - Bra

queue.match_reason: An open computer design that shaped digital culture connects with an episode reflecting on how our listening tools and platforms quietly rewire our relationship with music.

queue.fact_text: On this day in 1995, astronomers released the first image of the Sun from the SOHO spacecraft, revealing a dynamic, boiling star in unprecedented detail. Instead of a steady yellow disc, the Sun appeared as a restless sphere of loops, flares, and coronal holes, reminding us that our ‘calm’ daylight comes from a constantly exploding fusion engine.

episode.title: Morning Drops #14 - Slow Planet
episode.description: A pause. A quiet return to slower rhythms, sparked by a live set under the summer sky. A moment to breathe and settle into the week.

queue.match_reason: A spacecraft revealing the Sun’s hidden turbulence pairs with an episode about slowing down on a ‘quiet’ planet while knowing the cosmic engine above is anything but calm.

Despite early optical flaws, Hubble went on to revolutionize astronomy with crystal-clear images of distant galaxies, nebulae, and the expansion of the universe.
